key_points:
### Key Compliance Points — Responsible Business Conduct (Second Amendment) Directions, 2026
*(RBI/2026-27/122, dated 2026-06-15; effective 2027-01-01)*

Adds a comprehensive framework on **advertising, marketing and sale of financial products/services** (RBI/2026-27/122, 2026-06-15).

- **New definitions (Chapter I, para 3A):** *Compulsory bundling*, *Dark pattern*, *DSA/DMA* and *sub-agent*, *Explicit consent*, *Mis-selling* (unsuitable sale, incomplete/misleading info, no consent, compulsory bundling), *Third-party Product or Service (TPPS)*.
- **New Chapter IIIA – Advertising, Marketing and Sale:**
  - **Policy (32A–32B):** comprehensive sale/marketing policy incl. suitability criteria, feedback, mis-selling compensation, and DSA/DMA governance.
  - **DSAs/DMAs (32C–32F):** publish an up-to-date empanelled list (update within 7 days); required qualifications; on-person identification within premises; a **Code of Conduct** binding employees, DSAs/DMAs and TPPS reps.
  - **Consent (32G–32I):** sell only with **explicit consent** per product; disclose key features (fees, rate, risks, lock-in, exit penalties); consent flow must require reading T&Cs, with default choice **'No'**; preserve consent records for **1 year** after contract cessation.
  - **Advertising (32J–32O):** don't pass off TPPS as own; ads clear/factual with rates and charges; promotional comms only with consent; easy unsubscribe; contact **09:00–19:00** only; no misrepresentation/coercion; respect Do-Not-Disturb.
  - **Sale (32P–32X):** **suitability & appropriateness** assessment; product-specific/clearly-demarcated application forms; acknowledgements and copies of signed T&Cs; **no incentives that drive mis-selling**; **no compulsory bundling** of TPPS; no funding a purchase out of a loan without consent; **no dark patterns** (user testing + audit; adhere to CCPA dark-pattern guidelines).
  - **Feedback & compensation (32Y–32Z):** feedback mechanism within **30 days** of sale; complaint window (30 days where none specified); on established **mis-selling, refund the entire amount** and compensate for loss.
  - **Other regulators (32ZA):** comply with TRAI/DoT (TCCCPR), SEBI/IRDAI/PFRDA and related RBI guidelines.
- **Annex I:** illustrative **dark patterns** for AIFIs — false urgency, basket sneaking, confirm shaming, forced action, subscription trap, interface interference, bait and switch, drip pricing, disguised advertisement, nagging, trick wording.
- **Commencement:** **January 1, 2027.**

_Verify with the compliance team before acting._
what_changed:
### What Changed
*(Amendment: RBI/2026-27/122, dated 2026-06-15 · effective 2027-01-01)*

| Provision | Change type | Effect |
|---|---|---|
| **Chapter I, para 3A (Definitions)** | **Inserted** | Defines compulsory bundling, dark pattern, DSA/DMA, explicit consent, mis-selling, TPPS. |
| **New Chapter IIIA (32A–32ZA)** | **Inserted** | Full advertising/marketing/sale framework: policy, DSA/DMA governance + Code of Conduct, explicit consent, honest advertising, suitability assessment, anti-mis-selling, no dark patterns, feedback + full refund/compensation on mis-selling. |
| **Annex I** | **Inserted** | Illustrative list of dark patterns relevant to AIFIs. |

- **Effective date:** January 1, 2027.

_Verify with the compliance team before acting._
implications:
**Why it matters for NaBFID:** Although NaBFID is a wholesale infrastructure lender (not a retail bank), any product it markets or sells — including third-party products through agency arrangements — must now follow explicit-consent, suitability, honest-advertising and no-dark-pattern rules, with full refund + compensation if mis-selling is established.

*Illustrative example:* If NaBFID markets a third-party product to a borrower, it must obtain product-specific explicit consent, disclose all fees/risks upfront, avoid compulsory bundling, and cannot fund that purchase from a sanctioned loan without the borrower's consent — with records kept for a year and a feedback check within 30 days.

_Verify with the compliance team before acting._
